name: Mojito

spec.method_detail: Lightly muddle mint with syrup in glass (don't shred it — you want oils, not chlorophyll). Add lime, rum, and ice. Top with soda. Stir gently with bar spoon.

history: Cuba is the birthplace. The earliest ancestor was El Draque, a 16th-century mix of aguardiente, sugar, lime, and mint. The modern Mojito developed through the 19th and 20th centuries with lighter white rum (Bacardi became the go-to). La Bodeguita del Medio in Havana is its most famous home — Hemingway's quote 'My daiquiri at La Floridita, my mojito at La Bodeguita' is debated, but he definitely drank them.
